items.title: Elsa breaks new ground with autonomous underwater vehicle job for oil major
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Elsa Bhd has secured a contract to provide autonomous underwater vehicle (AUV) subsea structure inspection services for a major exploration and production (E&P) company across Peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak. Elsa claimed that this is a first-of-its-kind AUV deployment for subsea structure inspection involving vertical offshore platform structures in Malaysia. Under the contract, Elsa Energy will deploy AUV systems to carry out autonomous underwater inspections of offshore platform structures.

items.title: Chief statistician pays farewell visit to PM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today received a farewell courtesy visit from Chief Statistician of Malaysia Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin ahead of his mandatory retirement on July 8. In a Facebook post, the prime minister expressed his appreciation for Uzir's service and dedication in strengthening the national statistical system, which serves as a key foundation for policymaking and Malaysia's development planning. Anwar also shared photos of the visit, noting that Uzir was appointed Chief Statistician on Feb 14, 2017.

items.title: Use legal routes, Thai Army tells Malaysians travelling to southern Thailand
items.summary: RANTAU PANJANG: The Thai army has assured Malaysians travelling to southern Thailand that their safety remains a priority, while urging people from both countries to use only legal border crossings. Senior Expert of the Royal Thai Army and Chief of the Neighbouring Countries Coordination Centre, General Surathep Nukaeow, said the Thai military fully supports efforts by both Malaysia and Thailand to strengthen border security and curb illegal cross-border activities. He said authorities on both sides share the same policies and objectives in ensuring the border remains safe and secure.

items.title: Perkeso pays out RM1.2mil in first month of Lindung 24/7
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) has disbursed more than RM1.2 million in benefits under its Non-Employment Injury Scheme (Lindung 24 Hours), within the first month of its implementation. "On average, nearly 20 cases a day were recorded under Lindung 24 Hours, reflecting Perkeso's efforts to close gaps in Malaysia's social protection system. "Previously, accidents occurring outside working hours were not covered, but with Lindung 24 Hours, the situation has changed.

items.title: Car wash owner among 4 arrested over assault on foreign worker
items.summary: KUALA SELANGOR: Police have detained four people, including a car wash owner, after a foreign worker was assaulted at a car wash in Taman Kiara on Wednesday. Kuala Selangor police chief Superintendent Azaharudin Tajudin said investigations showed a Toyota Hilux driver struck the worker with a traffic cone and punched him several times. Azaharudin said following the incident, police detained the suspect, the car wash owner and two other foreign workers.

items.title: Nestlé Malaysia accelerates with KitKat F1 campaign
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Nestlé (Malaysia) Bhd has launched its KitKat Formula 1 campaign in the local market, offering consumers exclusive promotions, limited-edition products and opportunities to win tickets to the 2026 Formula 1 Singapore Grand Prix. The campaign is part of KitKat's global multi-year partnership with Formula 1, which coincides with the chocolate brand's 90th anniversary and Formula 1's 75th anniversary. Nestlé Malaysia chief executive officer Juan Aranols said Formula 1 has become a global cultural phenomenon, attracting a growing number of fans across Malaysia and the wider region.

items.title: TFP Solutions seals concert ticketing services with Pluotos Entertainment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: TFP Solutions Bhd has secured a concert ticketing services agreement with Pluotos Entertainment Sdn Bhd as it expands its presence in Malaysia's digital event technology and ticketing industry. The company said its wholly-owned Softfac Technology Sdn Bhd signed a one-year ticketing services and strategic collaboration agreement with Pluotos on July 2. TFP said the collaboration is in line with its strategy to expand its technology solutions business by strengthening its presence in the digital concert ticketing and event technology industry.

items.title: Titijaya Land eyes expansion in Sabah with new projects in pipeline
items.summary: The company is preparing to launch several new projects in Sabah, including a commercial development in Likas, Kota Kinabalu. Managing director Lim Poh Yit said the company remains optimistic about Sabah's long-term prospects following the positive response to its existing projects in the state, including The Shore and Citadines Waterfront Kota Kinabalu. Among Titijaya's flagship projects in the state is The Shore, a mixed-use development that includes residential, retail and hospitality components and houses Citadines Waterfront Kota Kinabalu.

items.title: High Court bars Chegubard from publishing statements on army general
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court has granted an interim injunction restraining activist Badrul Hisham Shaharin, better known as Chegubard, from publishing or disseminating statements concerning Major-General Faridhulathroth Hashim pending the disposal of a defamation suit filed against him. Faridhulathroth, 52, the assistant chief of staff (intelligence), filed the suit against Badrul Hisham, 48, on April 21. The suit arises from six allegedly defamatory statements published on Badrul Hisham's Facebook account between Dec 31, 2025 and Jan 17 concerning Faridhulathroth's alleged criminal activities and misconduct.

items.title: Court of Appeal upholds ruling that DAP’s Chong Chieng Jen defamed Sarawak government
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Court of Appeal has unanimously dismissed an appeal by Sarawak DAP chief Chong Chieng Jen, upholding a High Court ruling that he defamed the Sarawak government and the Sarawak Financial Authority (SFA) over claims regarding state funds in 2013. The three-judge panel increased the damages awarded to the state government and the SFA from RM150,000 to RM300,000. Chong said following the decision that he is considering filing an appeal to the Federal Court.

items.title: Transport Ministry reviewing taxi sector under Klang Valley transport plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Transport Ministry is implementing three proactive initiatives to rejuvenate the taxi industry, including a review under the Klang Valley public transport strategic plan. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the study would determine the future direction of the industry, including a review of taxi fares. He said the other measures involve developing a taxi vehicle financing package with the national car manufacturer and providing training for drivers.

items.title: Five Taiping Prison officers charged over alleged baton attacks on inmates
items.summary: TAIPING, July 3 — Five prison officers were charged in the Magistrate's Court here today with injuring three inmates at Taiping Prison in separate incidents last January. All of the accused — Mohd Hairie Jumri, 40; Saiful Azman Mohamad Ibrahim, 44; Ahmad Rizal Razali, 37; Muhamad Mustakhim Abdul Rahim, 40; and Nor Hafiz Izwan Muhammad Jajam, 31 — pleaded not guilty to the charges. In the court before Magistrate R. Prabakaran, Mohd Hairie and Saiful Azman are jointly charged with intentionally causing hurt to P. Jason Immanuel, 25, using a baton at Hall B, Taiping Prison, Jalan Taming Sari here, between 3pm and 4pm on Jan 17, 2025.

items.title: [UPDATE] Anwar: Any US pardon for Jho Low won't affect Malaysia's stance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said Malaysia will not change its position on Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, even if the fugitive businessman is granted a presidential pardon in the United States. "That is a decision for the US," Anwar said when asked about the possibility of a presidential pardon for Low, the alleged mastermind behind the multibillion-dollar 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) scandal. Anwar had previously said the government was not considering the possibility of granting a pardon to Low.

items.title: Only King can revoke Azam Baki's NFCC board appointment, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Only Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia has the authority to revoke Tan Sri Azam Baki's appointment as a member of the National Financial Crime Centre (NFCC) advisory board, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. Anwar said the appointment was made by the King and remains valid until 2027. "The appointment ends in 2027, and only the King can revoke it.

items.title: At least two dead in Russian border regions after Ukrainian drone attacks
items.summary: MOSCOW: At least two people were killed in Russia's border regions and several industrial sites suffered damage during Ukrainian drone attacks overnight, local authorities and media said on Friday. Russian President Vladimir Putin has said the attacks are meant to sow discord among the public. Separately, Ukrainian officials said on Telegram that four people were killed and a further 10 people were injured in Russian overnight attacks on Ukraine.

items.title: Shafee tells court Najib’s tax appeal would be moot if LHDN insists on ‘pay first, talk later’
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 3 — Lawyer Tan Sri Shafee Abdullah argued before the Court of Appeal today that failure to stay the Inland Revenue Board’s (LHDN) case against ex-prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak would render his appeal before the Special Commissioners of Income Tax (SCIT) in September “meaningless.” Speaking before a three-member bench chaired by Justice Datuk Alwi Abdul Wahab, Shafee contended that proceeding with the bankruptcy action would be “akin to entering the boxing ring with both hands tied,” Free Malaysia Today reported. The dispute centres on an additional tax assessment of RM1.69 billion for the 2011 to 2017 period, stemming from an alleged RM8 billion donation that Najib maintains was used for political and welfare activities. This hearing follows a November 17 ruling last year, in which the High Court dismissed applications by Najib and his son, Nazifuddin, to stay bankruptcy proceedings over unpaid tax arrears of RM1.69 billion and RM37.6 million, respectively.

items.title: Home minister: Police working with Interpol to locate Na’imah, children in Europe in ongoing probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) are tracking the whereabouts of the late Tun Daim Zainuddin’s widow, Toh Puan Na’imah Abdul Khalid, and her children, who are believed to be in Europe, to assist in an ongoing police investigation. “At this stage, PDRM is actively working to locate the late Tun Daim’s widow, Toh Puan Na’imah Abdul Khalid, and her children to assist in completing the ongoing police investigation. Saifuddin Nasution said PDRM had opened an investigation paper following Sentul Police Report No.

items.title: Stop 'old' attacks on Umno, BN, Zahid tells unity govt allies
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has urged his unity government allies not to raise old issues targeting Umno and the coalition during the Johor election campaign. He said each party should focus on highlighting the strengths of its own candidates instead of reviving old issues, given that cabinet ministers meet every week. Zahid also said BN was not overconfident about securing a landslide victory.

items.title: 4WD slips off deteriorating Baram logging bridge, renewing concerns over rural road safety
items.summary: MIRI, July 3 — A near-tragic incident involving a four-wheel-drive (4WD) vehicle on a deteriorating logging bridge along the road to SK Long Pelutan has renewed concerns over poor road conditions in the Baram interior. The incident occurred on Wednesday when the vehicle reportedly slipped off while crossing a damaged bridge along the route linking Long Lama and Miri, a road frequently used by teachers travelling to and from SK Long Pelutan. “The road conditions at the scene are extremely poor and dangerous.

items.title: Zero tolerance for wrongdoing, vows Prison Dept after 5 charged with assault
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Prisons Department will not compromise on any wrongdoing by its staff following the charging of five warders at the Taiping magistrate's court in Perak. Earlier today, the five warders pleaded not guilty to assaulting three inmates last year. Mohd Hairie Jumri, 40, and Saiful Azman Mohamad Ibrahim, 44, were charged under Section 324 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt using a dangerous weapon.

items.title: Najib, son to know on Sept 4 outcome of stay bid in tax case
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Datuk Seri Najib Razak and his son Datuk Nazifuddin Najib will know on Sept 4 whether the Court of Appeal will allow their bid to stay bankruptcy proceedings initiated by the Inland Revenue Board (IRB) over RM1.7 billion in unpaid taxes. The appeal is against the High Court's decision on Nov 17 last year dismissing Najib and Nazifuddin's application to stay the bankruptcy proceedings pending the disposal of their tax appeals before the Special Commissioners of Income Tax (SCIT). Senior lawyer Tan Sri Muhammad Shafee Abdullah, who represented the duo, argued that Najib's additional tax assessment of RM1.69 billion was imposed despite him having paid income tax.

items.title: Ex‑kindergarten teacher charged with laundering RM133,550 linked to alleged child‑relocation scheme to New Zealand
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 3 — A former kindergarten teacher pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to three counts of money laundering involving RM133,550 in cash and gold jewellery allegedly linked to a scheme promising to facilitate a child’s relocation to New Zealand last year. According to the charges, Venmani is accused of receiving proceeds from unlawful activities in three separate transactions involving RM83,550, RM50,000, two gold chains and a gold pendant from a 39-year-old local woman. The charges were framed under Section 4(1)(b) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (Act 613), punishable under Section 4(1) of the same Act.

items.title: Mesiniaga bags RM40mil job to provide centralised server infra
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Mesiniaga Bhd has secured a RM39.99 million contract from Pharmaniaga Logistics Sdn Bhd to deliver a new centralised server infrastructure for the Pharmacy Information System (PhIS) Version 3.0. The contract does not include an automatic renewal clause. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Singapore driver fined RM20,000 for filling RON95 petrol
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A foreign motorist was fined RM20,000, or three months' jail in default, by the Sessions Court here for illegally filling his Singapore-registered vehicle with subsidised RON95 petrol. Judge Datuk Che Wan Zaidi Che Wan Ibrahim passed the sentence yesterday after the accused, in his 50s and the driver and owner of the vehicle, pleaded guilty to the charge. The offence occurred on April 9, when Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry enforcement officers, conducting Op Tiris 4.0, caught the Singaporean filling his vehicle with BUDI95 at a petrol station here.

items.title: Penang to study order to disclose RM1bil Jelutong landfill agreement
items.summary: Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said any party disagreeing with the Freedom of Information (FOI) Appeals Board's ruling to disclose the agreement still had legal options. "The state government will study the issue first before making a decision," he said when asked if the state would make the agreement public. The state FOI Appeals Board on Tuesday ordered the state secretary's office to release the agreement after ruling that it did not fall under exempt documents.

items.title: Anwar boosts taxi replacement fund by RM10m, says dedicated Proton S70 financing in the works
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced an additional RM10 million allocation for the Vehicle Replacement Matching Grant Programme to help taxi drivers replace ageing vehicles. Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said the additional funding follows the encouraging response to the initial RM10 million allocation for the National Madani Taxi Reform Programme, announced in Budget 2026. Given the importance of the taxi industry, I have approved an immediate additional RM10 million specifically for taxi reform,” he said at the launch of the National Madani Taxi Reform Programme at Dataran Merdeka today.

items.title: Paris Baguette disposal clears earnings drag for Berjaya Food: CIMB Securities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Berjaya Food Bhd's disposal of its loss-making joint venture Berjaya Paris Baguette (BPB) is positive for its turnaround story, according to CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd. Berjaya Food on Tuesday disposed its 50 per cent stake, or 20 million shares, in Berjaya Paris Baguette to Paris Baguette Singapore, the franchisor and remaining 50 per cent shareholder, for a nominal RM1. CIMB Securities noted that Berjaya Food still retains exposure to other Paris Baguette-related ventures.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia extends midday gains
items.summary: Among heavyweight stocks, Maybank gained 12 sen to RM10.86, CIMB added 10 sen to RM7.55, Tenaga Nasional increased four sen to RM14.30, and Public Bank was one sen higher at RM4.85, while IHH Healthcare was flat at RM8.17. As for the active stocks, Velesto bagged 2.5 sen to 29 sen, Eckem Holdings inched up half a sen to 12.5 sen, Zetrix AI increased 1.5 sen to 79.5 sen, Silver Ridge gained four sen to 21 sen, while Tanco was flat at 17.5 sen. On the gainers list, Binastra climbed eight sen to RM2.14, F&N jumped 68 sen to RM28.78, KL Kepong added 52 sen to RM21.48, Dutch Lady soared 24 sen to RM32.30, and PPB bagged 16 sen to RM9.49. Of the decliners, Nestle shed 92 sen to RM91.08, Malaysian Pacific Industries lost 32 sen to RM47.74, United Plantations declined 22 sen to RM33.26, Far East dropped nine sen to RM3.81, and Gas Malaysia trimmed seven sen to RM5.13.

items.title: Armizan: Applications open today for diesel‑subsidy scheme for company jeeps, pick‑ups; payments start July 15
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 3 — Applications for the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) have opened today for the land transport sector covering company-owned private vehicles, including jeeps and pick-up trucks, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said. “To qualify, vehicles must be registered as business vehicles under the Company Private Use (AE) class/code in the Road Transport Department’s (JPJ) MySikap system. The government had previously extended SKDS eligibility to two other categories: the public land transport sector and the consumer goods land transport sector.

items.title: Chinese robot maker Unitree wins approval for US$619 million Shanghai IPO
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Chinese robot maker Unitree Robotics has received regulatory approval for a Shanghai listing through which it plans to raise 4.2 billion yuan (US$619.4 million), moving it closer to one of China's most watched technology initial public offerings. The approval comes as China's onshore IPO market shows signs of life after a long slowdown. Unitree plans to use the proceeds for robot artificial intelligence model research, robot body research, new robot product development and a smart robot manufacturing base, according to the prospectus.

items.title: PLUS: Smart Lane at Bandar Saujana Putra-Putra Heights to close for five months for maintenance
items.summary: PLUS Malaysia Berhad (PLUS) in a statement today said the temporary closure is to facilitate maintenance works as part of its ongoing efforts to improve the highway infrastructure and ensure the safety of highway users. “Throughout the maintenance works, the existing three lanes at the affected stretch will remain open to maintain traffic flow and to minimise traffic disruptions for highway users,” the statement read. PLUS also encouraged highway users to plan their journeys using the PLUS app for the latest traffic updates and obey all traffic signage and instructions issued by PLUS personnel when passing through the construction zone.

items.title: Johor to expand Bangsa Johor school initiative to religious schools
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor is expanding its Sekolah Rintis Bangsa Johor (SRBJ) initiative to religious schools as part of efforts to transform Islamic education in the state. Caretaker Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the Bangsa Johor Religious Pioneer School initiative would complement the four SRBJ schools already in operation. Meanwhile, caretaker Johor Islamic religious affairs committee chairman Mohd Fared Mohd Khalid said the first Bangsa Johor religious school would be located in Kota Iskandar, Iskandar Puteri, and would begin operations this year.

items.title: China stocks rebound after selloff; Hong Kong extends gains on Fed rate outlook
items.summary: As of midday, the Shanghai Composite index inched up 0.6 per cent, while the blue-chip CSI300 index gained 1.2 per cent. The tech-focused STAR 50 index, which suffered its biggest one-day decline since April 2025 on Thursday, advanced 1.1 per cent. In Hong Kong, the Hang Seng Index advanced 1.6 per cent, while the city's tech shares jumped 2 per cent.

items.title: 5,140 of Malaysia's new passports issued since Wednesday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department has issued 5,140 copies of the new Malaysian passport since Wednesday. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said applications at 14 passport issuing offices nationwide remained manageable. The new passport features 94 security characteristics, up from 49 previously.

items.title: New taxi financing model ends leasing system, says Loke
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Taxi drivers participating in the National Madani Taxi Reform Programme will now own their vehicles outright under a new financing model, replacing the long-standing leasing system. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said a special approval from the Finance Ministry allows drivers to become the legal owners of their new taxis, marking the end of the traditional leasing practice in the industry. "This is not the old scheme or the old leasing model.

items.title: Executor of late Waz Lian co-founder Ta Kin Yan’s estate says will is clear, leaves no room for dispute
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The executor and trustee of the late Tan Sri Ta Kin Yan’s estate said the late businessman’s will is clear in its intent and leaves no room for dispute. Datuk Poh Po Lian, who co-founded the Waz Lian Group with Ta in 1987, said Ta’s will — which had been kept in a bank safe — provides a clear and equitable framework for administering the estate. The will, dated February 4, 2008, appoints Poh as the executor and trustee of Ta’s estate.

items.title: Proton S70 named official taxi under Madani reform
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Proton S70 has been selected as the official taxi model under the National Madani Taxi Reform Programme, marking a key shift in the modernisation of the country's taxi industry. He said this was part of broader efforts to enhance the image and competitiveness of the taxi industry. He said the reform programme also introduces a new financing model that allows drivers to own their vehicles outright, replacing the long-standing leasing system.

items.title: PH wants Johor to become economic powerhouse, not just 'feeder' territory
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan wants Johor to emerge as an economic powerhouse instead of merely as a "feeder" territory to Singapore. Amirudin said Johor must become a state capable of generating its own opportunities, talent, and wealth. Also present were Johor PH chairman and state Amanah chairman Aminolhuda Hassan, Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching, and Johor PKR chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa.

items.title: Foreign woman reported missing since June 30 found dead in Penampang apartment; police rule out foul play
items.summary: PENAMPANG, July 3 — A 42-year-old foreign woman who had been reported missing since June 30 was found dead in her apartment along Jalan Penampang Lama. Penampang police chief Supt Sammy Newton said the victim, who lived alone, was found by her employer who had become concerned when repeated attempts to contact her since that morning went unanswered. “Upon arriving at the residence, the employer found the apartment door locked and received no response from the victim, and proceeded to contact the authorities.

items.title: [UPDATED] PM: RM10mil approved for old taxi replacement scheme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has approved an additional RM10 million for the matching grant programme to replace old taxis, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced today. Anwar, who is also the finance minister, said the additional allocation would support the National MADANI Taxi Reform Programme, which is expected to raise drivers' monthly net income through lower repair, maintenance, servicing and financing costs. "There was one point raised, which is taxi income.

items.title: India's HCLTech wins US$1.14 billion deal with European firm
items.summary: BENGALURU: India's HCLTech has won a US$1.14 billion deal with a major European firm, the software services exporter said on Friday. Here are some details: The deal will help HCLTech establish an AI-driven operating model to transform and manage the client's global digital workplace and enterprise networks, the company said. This is entirely new business for the company, HCLTech said, and not from an existing client or a deal renewal.

items.title: Anti-financial crime body says Azam Baki’s board role is in ‘individual capacity’, not tied to MACC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The National Financial Crime Prevention Centre (NFCC) today insisted that Tan Sri Azam Baki was appointed to its advisory board in his individual capacity, and not for his former position as MACC chief commissioner. “Therefore, his membership in the NFCC advisory board is not tied to the position of chief commissioner of the MACC and remains in force until the appointment expires, unless terminated earlier by the Yang di-Pertuan Agong,” the NFCC said. The NFCC also clarified that Azam’s term is valid for three years, running from September 2024 to September 2027.

items.title: Gempur Rasuah Sabah organisers confirm rally route, cite Federal Court ruling in decision not to submit police notice
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 3 — Organisers of the Gempur Rasuah Sabah 3.0 anti-corruption protest have confirmed that participants will assemble at Suria Sabah on Saturday before marching through the city centre to Dataran Todak Waterfront. In a statement released ahead of the event, the organisers announced that the gathering would begin at 2pm in front of Suria Sabah, followed by a procession passing several checkpoints including the Kota Kinabalu flyover, Centre Point, Anjung Kinabalu and Pasar Besar Kota Kinabalu before concluding at Dataran Todak Waterfront. The organisers also confirmed that they would not be submitting a notice of assembly to the police, citing a Federal Court decision delivered in June 2025 which declared Section 9(5) of the Peaceful Assembly Act 2012 unconstitutional.

items.title: Pakatan outlines vision for state with ‘Johor Untuk Semua’ manifesto
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) has unveiled its Johor election manifesto, ‘Johor Untuk Semua’, a strategic blueprint centred on modernising the state and securing its future. “The manifesto is not an election document, rather it is a roadmap to make Johor successful,” Maszlee said. Earlier in his speech, Amirudin said the PH manifesto offer was to elevate and empower Johor as a perfect and complete state.

items.title: Govt to build new Bandar Enstek hospital as MOH moves to ease HTJ congestion and expand services, says Dzulkefly
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — A new hospital will be built in Bandar Enstek, Nilai, Negeri Sembilan, to meet the healthcare needs of residents in the Seremban district while easing congestion at the Tuanku Ja’afar Hospital. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the proposed hospital in the northern Seremban corridor was appropriate in view of the area’s rapid development, which had directly contributed to a sharp increase in population. He said the decision to build the new hospital followed the Health Ministry (MOH)’s review of the proposed Tuanku Ja’afar Hospital 2 (HTJ2) project in Rasah, as well as discussions with Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun on June 16.

items.title: High Court sets October trial dates for Nhaveen murder case following overturned acquittal
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 3 — The High Court here has scheduled eight trial dates in October for four men charged with the murder of teenager T Nhaveen nine years ago. Deputy Public Prosecutor Amril Johari confirmed that Judge Radzi Abdul Hamid will preside over the trial from October 7 to 9, and again from October 12 to 16. A case mention has been set for July 13, following proceedings conducted via Zoom today, Free Malaysia Today reported.

items.title: Johor polls: BN’s Aznan pledges to boost eco‑tourism, oil and gas sectors in Tanjung Surat
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 3 — The incumbent assemblyman for Tanjung Surat, Aznan Tamin, is determined to strengthen the eco-tourism and oil and gas sectors as key drivers of economic growth in the constituency if given the mandate again in the 16th Johor State Election. Aznan said Tanjung Surat’s strategic location near the Pengerang oil and gas industrial hub also provides opportunities to create more high-income jobs, especially for young people. N39: Tanjung Surat — 26,943 1.

items.title: Not just a camera: Anthony Loke explains the ‘ecosystem’ behind AwAS maintenance costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke has corrected claims regarding the maintenance costs of the Automated Awareness Safety System (AwAS), clarifying that the actual annual cost for a single camera unit is approximately RM112,800 and not the RM337,000 figure frequently cited. In a written parliamentary reply yesterday, Loke explained that the RM112,800 figure covers essential preventive maintenance, repairs, and periodic calibration. He said that the higher, commonly referenced total actually reflects the system’s entire operational ecosystem rather than the maintenance of individual cameras.

items.title: The future of campaigning? Johor election becomes Malaysian politics biggest generative AI test bed
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — The Johor state election is becoming Malaysia’s biggest test bed for generative AI. As 2.7 million voters prepare to head to the polls on July 11, political teams from Pakatan Harapan (PH), Barisan Nasional (BN), and Parti Bersama Malaysia are increasingly replacing traditional production with AI-generated anthems, social media posts, and rally planning. Adib Aiman, a PH content creator who has generated hundreds of songs using the AI music generator Suno, noted the efficiency of the transition.

items.title: Johor polls: Ling Tian Soon banks on ‘Jalan Koyok’ upgrade as key achievement in Yong Peng
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, July 3 — Once mocked by residents as ‘Jalan Koyok’ owing to its heavily patched condition, the road transformation has now become one of the highlights in Yong Peng state assemblyman Ling Tian Soon’s report card in his bid to secure a second-term mandate in the Johor state polls. Ling, 42, who is also the former state Health and Environment Committee chairman, said the transformation has begun to boost investor confidence, with more major logistics and courier companies choosing Yong Peng, located about 100 kilometres from Johor Bahru city centre, as their operational base. N19: Yong Peng: 34,023 1.

items.title: Police open eight probe papers in Johor polls, five involving arson‑related mischief
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — Police have opened eight investigation papers linked to the 16th Johor state election for the period from June 27 until yesterday. State police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said five of the cases involve arson-related mischief and other acts causing property damage. Ab Rahaman said the security situation throughout the election period has remained peaceful and under control, with 2,037 permit applications received and 1,849 approved so far.

items.title: Mosti to prioritise talent development and support TVET strengthening ahead of AMMSTI‑23, says minister
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — The Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation (Mosti) will place talent development at the forefront of its agenda as Malaysia prepares to host the 23rd Asean Ministerial Meeting on Science, Technology and Innovation (AMMSTI-23) next year. “Besides talent development, our focus areas include energy transition, artificial intelligence (AI), digitalisation, advanced materials, nanotechnology, including hydrogen, and biotechnology,” he said. In another development, Chang said Mosti remains committed to supporting other ministries in strengthening Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) programmes to better meet current industry demands, although TVET does not fall under the ministry’s direct jurisdiction.

items.title: Police classify roadside discovery of ex‑prisoner’s body near Machang as sudden death
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 3 — Police classified the discovery of a man’s body by the side of the Kota Bharu-Kuala Krai road, near Machang, yesterday afternoon as a sudden death case, after an initial examination found no criminal elements. Machang district police chief Supt Azuhar Md Nor said his team received information about the discovery of the man’s body at around 3.47pm, lying face down on the grass verge at Kilometre 25 Jalan Kota Bharu-Kuala Krai. An initial examination by the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) forensic team of the Kelantan police contingent headquarters (IPK) found no signs of injuries with criminal elements,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Miti: Govt confident Sabah, Sarawak can attract quality investments through downstream industries and strategic industrial parks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The government is confident that Sabah and Sarawak will continue to be competitive investment destinations through the development of natural resource-based downstream industries, the strengthening of strategic industrial areas, and increased participation of micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) in the supply chain. The Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (Miti) said this would simultaneously create more quality job opportunities, retain local talent in rural and remote areas, and contribute to more balanced and inclusive economic development. “Miti, through its agency the Malaysian Investment Development Authority (MIDA), remains committed to attracting high-quality foreign and domestic investments to Sabah and Sarawak, particularly in natural resource-based industries,” it said in a written reply to the Dewan Rakyat today.

items.title: Thunderstorm alert out for Negeri Sembilan, Melaka, Johor, Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for parts of Negeri Sembilan, Melaka, Johor, and Sarawak, urging residents to prepare for heavy rain, thunderstorms, and strong winds. The affected areas include: Negeri Sembilan: Seremban, Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah, Rembau, and Tampin. Melaka: Entire state.

items.title: RM1 ATM fee: How to distinguish free bank ATMs from paid non-bank machines
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — While the RM1 interbank ATM withdrawal fee was waived on 1 July 2026, banks clarified today that users must still distinguish between bank-operated and non-bank tellers to avoid unexpected charges. According to a joint statement from The Association of Banks in Malaysia (ABM) and others, the fee waiver applies exclusively to ATMs operated by banks nationwide. “Bank-operated ATMs clearly display a single bank’s name and logo on the exterior of the ATM and on the ATM screen,” the groups said, However, standard fees may still apply when using non-bank operated ATMs, which generally display the logos of multiple different banks.

items.title: Miti: Stronger ringgit boosts Malaysia’s export competitiveness and reduces import‑driven production costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The strengthening of the ringgit has not affected the country’s trade performance. Instead, it has helped to make Malaysia’s exports more price-competitive in international markets, according to the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (Miti). Malaysia’s trade performance for the January-May 2026 period increased 18.3 per cent to RM1.455 trillion compared with the same period last year.

items.title: Nga Kor Ming: Govt offers 10pc home‑purchase discount at AREC 2026; Malaysian developers win 14 FIABCI awards
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The government is offering a 10 per cent discount on home purchases during the Asean Real Estate Conference (AREC) 2026, which will be held at the Malaysia International Trade and Exhibition Centre (MITEC) from July 29 to August 1. Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the initiative, introduced through collaboration between the ministry and the Real Estate and Housing Developers’ Association Malaysia (REHDA), is aimed at easing the financial burden on homebuyers, particularly in meeting the 10 per cent deposit required under the Sale and Purchase Agreement (SPA). “The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) has also introduced the Rahmah Cement initiative to help the industry address rising construction costs by providing 1.6 million metric tonnes of cement for developers building affordable homes.

items.title: Thai authorities detain suspect in roadside bombing that injured two Malaysians
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — Thai security forces have apprehended a local man to assist in the investigation of a roadside bombing at Sapom Junction, Tak Bai District, which left two Malaysians injured last Monday. A source from the Internal Security Operations Command Region 4 (ISOC 4) confirmed that the arrest was triggered by targeted intelligence. “The suspect was taken for further investigation at the 46th Ranger Regiment Camp in Mueang Narathiwat District after authorities found an unusual quantity of items inconsistent with the circumstances of local residents,” the source said yesterday.

items.title: Fahmi says RTM prepared to host Maszlee-Onn Hafiz dialogue on Johor’s development agenda
items.summary: MUAR, July 3 — Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) is ready to provide a live broadcast platform for an interactive debate should Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi agree to face Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Puteri Wangsa state seat, Dr Maszlee Malik. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the dialogue could be held at any time and at any mutually agreed venue, including in Johor Bahru, with discussions focusing on Johor’s development agenda and future. If necessary, I am ready to provide a platform on RTM TV1 for Dr Maszlee and Datuk Onn Hafiz to hold a dialogue.
